race.name: Bieg Bystrzycki
race.tagline: Twenty-five kilometers through the Sudeten mountain valleys — a Polish classic where Silesian grit meets alpine-grade climbing.

vitals.aid_stations: 2 feed stations with warm tea and energy bars

climate.description: Early February in the Sudeten mountains of southwestern Poland. The moderate altitude provides decent snow conditions when cold air masses settle over Central Europe, but warm spells from the west can cause rapid thaws. Temperatures typically -6 to 0 degrees Celsius.
challenges: Moderate altitude makes snow cover variable year to year
Exposed ridgeline sections with wind chill
Freeze-thaw cycles create icy patches on shaded descents
Short daylight hours require early starts

course.primary: A 25km classic course through the Sudeten mountain valleys around Bystrzyca Kłodzka. The route features notable climbing through forested hillsides with some technical descents. The terrain is more demanding than the distance suggests, with the Sudeten foothills providing genuine mountain character.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through mountain forest and valley trails
course.grooming: Regional grooming standard with attention to technical descents
features: Genuine mountain character in the Sudeten foothills
Forested climbs with panoramic ridge views
Technical descents requiring solid classic technique
Passes near the historic gold mining town of Bystrzyca Kłodzka
Compact distance with concentrated elevation gain

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Elevated course technicality score for genuine mountain terrain in a short format. Limited by small field, local-only profile, and variable snow at moderate altitude.

history.summary: Founded in 1992 shortly after the fall of communism in Poland, the Bieg Bystrzycki emerged from the strong Silesian cross-country skiing tradition. Bystrzyca Kłodzka, a historic town in the Kłodzko Valley, provided a natural setting for a mountain-oriented Nordic race. The event has remained a beloved regional fixture, drawing skiers from across southwestern Poland to the Sudeten mountains.
notable_facts: Founded in 1992, one of the first post-communist era Polish ski races
Bystrzyca Kłodzka is a historic fortified town dating to the 13th century
The Sudeten mountains straddle the Polish-Czech border
Part of the Lower Silesian regional ski race circuit
The Kłodzko Valley is one of Poland's most scenic winter landscapes
